title: Noun Gender and Definite Articles
description: Learn how Bulgarian nouns work with gender, definite forms, and a few common patterns.

content.title: Why Gender Matters
content.description: Bulgarian nouns are usually masculine, feminine, or neuter, and the form of the article depends on that gender.

content.title: Big idea
content.text: Unlike English, Bulgarian usually puts the definite article after the noun: ученикът, книгата, морето.

content.title: What to watch for
bullets: Learn the noun together with its gender.
Check the ending, but do not rely on the ending alone.
Practice the definite form as a whole word, not as a separate article.

content.title: Common Gender Patterns
content.description: Use the tabs to compare the main gender shapes one at a time.

content.title: Pattern box
content.description: Switch between the three common noun pattern families.

tabs.label: Masculine
tabs.description: Masculine nouns often end in a consonant.

tabs.label: Feminine
tabs.description: Feminine nouns often end in -а or -я.

tabs.label: Neuter
tabs.description: Neuter nouns often end in -о or -е.

content.note: Neuter nouns often take -то in the definite form.

content.title: Definite Forms and Usage
content.description: Continue after the pattern box with the article forms and a few full sentences.

content.note: A definite masculine noun can still behave differently depending on sentence role.
This sentence mixes definite and indefinite forms in one line.

content.title: Quick study cue
items: Say the gender and the noun together.
Switch to the definite form only after the base form feels automatic.
Use the article in a sentence instead of memorizing it alone.
